repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 Manski nonparametric bounds endpoint only

published_prior_art.cite: Robins1986 g-formula endpoint; DiemerShiSwanson2024 assumption-free sustained-strategy bounds
KimKwonKwonLee2018: Lipschitz/smoothness partial-identification bounds for counterfactual outcomes; DiemerShiSwanson2024: dynamic sustained-strategy bounds
BojinovRambachanShephard2021: dynamic causal effects for alternative treatment paths in panel experiments; Han2021 finite dynamic treatment identification

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The cited LeeShin2016 entry omits Jun from the actual Jun, Lee, and Shin (2016) JBES paper, making the body citation/bibkey unstable.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest Lipschitz partial-ID comparator is missing: Kim, Kwon, Kwon, and Lee (2018, Quantitative Economics) study sharp counterfactual-outcome bounds under Lipschitz smoothness assumptions.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The dynamic treatment-history literature is thin: Bojinov, Rambachan, and Shephard (2021, Quantitative Economics) is a closer path-effect/carryover comparator than several DID endpoint papers.

novelty_flags.label: Proposal tier
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to reach flagship, the proposal needs an explicit strict-extension claim over the Lipschitz partial-ID literature or a named open dynamic-carryover regime.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup
soundness_flags.one_line: The observed support set O_t(h_t) is not typed precisely: H_t includes past treatments/outcomes, while O_t(h_t) is said to share only the covariate component, so mu_t^P(w) and d_sp(v,w) are not unambiguously defined on the same objects.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Corollary 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The rho=infty endpoint does not specify whether consistency anchors at factual sustained-history nodes are retained; the formula ell=0,u=1 and the [-1,1] Manski reduction require that convention to be explicit.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary 3
soundness_flags.one_line: For absorbing paths, d_sp becomes a Hamming distance between switch-boundary vectors plus current-treatment mismatch, not ordinary adoption-time distance.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked observable law P is a finite-T panel law, but the long-run Cesaro statement and stationary Bellman equations require an infinite stationary process or a compatible sequence P_T.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Kim, Kwon, Kwon, and Lee (2018) as the main Lipschitz/smoothness partial-ID comparator and state exactly why a reversible treatment-history metric is a strict dynamic extension rather than a relabeling.
Repair the rho=infty endpoint convention by specifying whether factual consistency anchors survive at observed sustained nodes, then restate the Manski reduction accordingly.
Replace the absorbing-path sanity check with the correct boundary-vector Hamming reduction, or define a calendar adoption-time metric if that is the intended object.

To lift toward flagship, formulate Conjecture 1 as a strict extension of a named Lipschitz partial-ID theorem to a finite-state dynamic regime and cite an explicit open gap in carryover/reversal settings.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal around the missed Lipschitz partial-ID prior art and fix the support-set, infinity-endpoint, and absorbing-metric definitions before another novelty pass.
